title: Purity sorting HEK293F cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8b7qn","text":"This protocol covers the sorting of landing pad cells (HEK293F) for purity prior to starting MultiSTEP experiments. This avoids using cells which have undergone landing pad silencing, and increases the proportion of cells that are recombined during transfection experiments. Cells are sorted on blue fluorescence, as cells which do express the landing pad also express blue fluorescent protein (BFP). Sort ~2 million BFP+ cells and plate in 10 mL with doxycycline and grow up.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"f47i8","text":"Ensure that the culture of cells has been growing for several days before purity sorting - do not use freshly thawed cells in this protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7j9u","text":"Cells should be sorted using an 85-um nozzle for a 4-way sterile purity sort (these are flow cytometer settings).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Transfer suspension culture to an appropriately-sized conical tube (e.g. 15 mL for a 10 mL culture). Save 1 mL as a backup in case the sort fails or the sorted cells become contaminated. Fill the flask with media (e.g. 9 mL media for 10 mL culture) and return to the shaking incubator.
Prepare several flow tubes for recovery of sorted cells - label and fill with 2 mL suspension media.
Resuspend the pellet in ¼-⅓ of original volume of flow buffer and transfer to a 5-mL flow tube.
Aspirate the media.
Centrifuge at 300 x g, °C, 5 minutes .
On the flow cytometer software, open your experiment and create a tube within that experiment for the cells.

As the flow cytometer sorts the cells, pay attention to the volumes in both the original flow tube and the recovery tube. Do NOT let the original flow tube run empty, or the recovery tube overflow. To stop the sorter, click on “Pause” in the sort layout window - this does not stop the sorter from pulling from the original flow tube. Click on “Stop Acquisition” or “Unload” if it is necessary to change the tube on the sample loader.
Remove the cap and load the flow tube containing cells into the sample loader. Press “Load” on the software. Analysis should begin automatically, or press “Acquire data”.
Click on the “Sort” button in the sort layout window.

Gate for single cells by gating in the following order: FSC-A vs SSC-A to gate for live cells FSC-A vs FSC-H to gate for single cells in the forward direction SSC-A vs SSC-H to gate for single cells in the side direction
Choose the stall which contains the recovery tube, and select the population to sort into the recovery tube (the BFP-expressing cells gated in go to step #15 ).

Create a graph that shows BFP fluorescence on the x-axis and choose “histogram” on the y-axis. Adjust the BFP laser setting if needed so that the main peak (with strong BFP fluorescence) is centered over 10^5.
In the software’s experiment dashboard, open the “Cytometer settings”, then open a sort layout.
Install the recovery tube in the sorter.

Gate for cells that fluoresce BFP - select a tight area around the positive peak. This will be the sorted population. Note the percentage of the single cell population that expresses BFP. Click on “Record data”. If the percentage is >98%, sorting is not necessary.
Remove tubes from the cytometer and follow instructions for post-sorting cleanup given by the manager of the flow cytometer.
Sort cells into the prepared recovery tube until the total number of cells needed is reached (approximately 2 million).
Centrifuge the recovery tubes at 300 x g, °C, 5 minutes .
Add the resuspended cells to the prepared flask.
Resuspend the cells in a total of 1 mL suspension media (for 2 recovery tubes add 500 µL to each, etc.).
Aspirate the supernatant from the recovery tubes.

While the centrifuge is running, prepare a new flask for the desired volume of culture by adding N- 1 mL of Freestyle 293 media to the flask (for a desired 10 mL culture, add 9 mL to the flask now). Add doxycycline and blasticidin to the flask.
Place the flask in a 37 °C shaking incubator with 8% CO 2 and check cells until confluence.
Optional: estimate the number of cells by staining the cells with trypan and counting using a hemocytometer.
creator: Sarah Heidl
